name: Avesta Housing Development Corporation
description_short: Maine & New Hampshire nonprofit providing affordable housing development, property management, and HUD-certified financial counseling to renters and homeowners at risk of eviction or foreclosure.
description_long:
Avesta Housing Development Corporation is a nonprofit organization operating across Maine and New Hampshire that addresses housing affordability and stability through multiple integrated programs. Founded on the mission that everyone deserves a place to call home, the organization manages 100 affordable properties containing over 3,000 apartments while also providing supportive services to vulnerable populations.

Avesta's primary services include multifamily affordable housing development and rehabilitation, property management with on-site support staff, HUD-certified financial counseling and credit counseling, eviction prevention assistance, homeownership education and first-time homebuyer support, residential care and assisted living for older adults, and specialized housing programs for formerly homeless individuals and chronic homeless populations. Their Financial Counseling & Homeownership department serves both Avesta residents and the broader community, offering free services to those facing housing instability.

What distinguishes Avesta is their comprehensive, wraparound service model that combines housing with resident support services. Beyond basic property management, they operate specialized programs including Homeless to Housing (in partnership with Maine Behavioral Healthcare), Home for Good communities providing 24/7 support to chronically homeless individuals, food security partnerships, recent immigrant services, and annual resident scholarships for post-secondary education. Their HUD-certified counselors provide eviction and foreclosure prevention at no charge, helping residents understand credit, design budgets, negotiate with lenders, and navigate legal processes.

Avesta is most credible for genuine nonprofit housing and financial counseling services rather than credit repair or debt relief. Their strength lies in integrated community support, but their services are geographically limited to Maine and New Hampshire. Consumers outside these states or seeking credit repair specifically would need to look elsewhere.
pros: HUD-certified financial counselors provide free eviction and foreclosure prevention services including credit counseling, budget design, and lender negotiation
Operates 100 properties with 3,000+ affordable apartments, demonstrating substantial real-world housing impact
Comprehensive wraparound services including food security partnerships, homeless transition programs, and resident scholarships
Specialized programs for vulnerable populations: chronic homelessness (Home for Good), recent immigrants, and formerly homeless individuals
On-site trained staff at all properties including property managers, resident service coordinators, and maintenance technicians
Financial counseling available to community members beyond just residents
24/7 support services at Home for Good communities ensuring continuous assistance for chronically homeless populations
cons: Services limited to Maine and New Hampshire only—not accessible to consumers in other states
Not a credit repair company; cannot dispute errors or negative items on credit reports
Primarily focused on housing-adjacent services rather than standalone credit building or credit monitoring tools
Financial counseling emphasis on eviction/foreclosure prevention may limit scope compared to comprehensive credit counseling agencies
No mention of credit cards, secured cards, or credit builder loan products on website
best_for: Maine and New Hampshire renters at risk of eviction who need free eviction prevention counseling and budget planning
Homeowners facing mortgage delinquency or foreclosure in Maine and New Hampshire seeking HUD-certified foreclosure counseling
Formerly homeless or chronically homeless individuals seeking permanent supportive housing with wraparound services
Recent immigrants in Maine and New Hampshire needing affordable housing combined with community support services
diagnosis: Avesta Housing is best for Maine and New Hampshire residents facing eviction, foreclosure, or homelessness who need integrated affordable housing with free HUD-certified financial counseling and wraparound support services. The main caveat is geographic limitation to two states and positioning as a housing nonprofit rather than a credit-specific agency—consumers seeking credit repair, credit monitoring, or credit-building products should consult other providers.
services: Multifamily affordable housing development and rehabilitation
Property management of 100 affordable housing properties
HUD-certified financial counseling (free for residents and community)
Credit counseling and personal finance education
Eviction prevention assistance and action planning
Foreclosure prevention counseling and mortgage company negotiation
First-time homebuyer education and purchasing assistance
Homeless to Housing program with transition specialists
Home for Good permanent supportive housing with 24/7 support services
Food security assistance through food bank partnerships
Recent immigrant housing and community support services
Resident scholarships for post-secondary education programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
